_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of cybersecurity and cybersecurity-enabled software products.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI application security risks, including prompt injection, jailbreaking, data exfiltration via LLM outputs, and model supply chain vulnerabilities.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-012 (SP, cosine: 0.471), DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.471).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.9/10 (raw 0.492). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [cybersecurity, enabled, products, software] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, application, chain, data, exfiltration, injection] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.875 (P=0.910, R=0.843). P exceeds R by 0.067 — the DCWF element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (90.1%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of cybersecurity and cybersecurity-enabled software products." and "Knowledge of AI application security risks, including prompt injection, jailbrea". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.493 ≈ STS 0.492 (gap 0.002). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [knowledge] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.5%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [cybersecurity, enabled, products, software]. WIDAI-only: [ai, application, chain, data, exfiltration, injection].
